title: Aloo Chaat

ingredients.title: For the Potatoes
items: 500 g (about 4 medium) potatoes – peeled and cut into 2 cm cubes
2 tbsp ghee (clarified butter) or neutral oil
½ tsp Kashmiri red chili powder

ingredients.title: For the Chaat Assembly
items: 1 green chili – finely chopped (optional, for heat)
2 tbsp fresh coriander leaves – finely chopped
2 tbsp fresh mint leaves – finely chopped
2 tbsp sweetened yogurt (optional, for Delhi-style chaat)
1 tbsp sev (crispy chickpea flour noodles) – for garnish

instruction.title: Prepare the Potatoes
steps: Bring a pot of salted water to a boil. Add potato cubes and cook until just tender (about 8–10 min).
Drain well, spread on a tray, and let them dry slightly for 5 minutes — this ensures crispness when frying.

instruction.title: Pan-Fry the Potatoes
steps: Heat ghee or oil in a heavy skillet over medium-high heat.
Add cumin seeds and let them crackle (10 seconds).
Add potatoes, sprinkle with salt, chili powder, roasted cumin powder, and chaat masala.
Sauté until golden and crisp on all sides (6–8 minutes). Remove from heat.

instruction.title: Assemble the Chaat
steps: Place crispy potatoes in a wide mixing bowl.
Toss gently with onion, tomato, green chili, coriander, and mint.
Drizzle with tamarind chutney, green chutney, and yogurt (if using).
Taste and adjust seasoning with extra chaat masala or salt.

instruction.title: Garnish & Serve
steps: Plate the chaat, sprinkle with sev and pomegranate seeds.
Serve immediately — aloo chaat loses its magic if it sits too long.

chefTips: Texture contrast is key — potatoes must be crisp outside, fluffy inside. Letting boiled potatoes air-dry before frying is essential.
Balance sweet, sour, spicy, and salty — chutneys bring sweetness and tang, chaat masala adds a zesty punch, chili gives heat.
Advance prep — Chutneys can be made up to 3 days ahead and refrigerated.
Professional plating — Use a shallow bowl, arrange potatoes in a nest, drizzle chutneys in a zigzag pattern, top with micro herbs and sev for a refined look.
